summary: Dalam pemilihan utama Demokrat Maine, Graham Platter yang terlibat skandal mengalahkan Gabenor Janet Mills dengan kira-kira 75% undi, dan akan mencabar Senator Republikan Susan Collins. Platter perlu meraih sokongan penyokong Mills, manakala Collins masih mempunyai kelebihan dalam kalangan pengundi bebas. Berita lain: AS melancarkan serangan peluru berpandu ke atas Iran; OpenAI memohon IPO; pendengaran fail Epstein; kenyataan kontroversi calon gabenor Colorado Victor Marx.

vocabulary.en: to continue to support or choose someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
vocabulary.example_cn: 缅因州民主党人投票决定继续支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。

vocabulary.cn: 翻转（指政党在选举中赢得原本由对手控制的席位）
vocabulary.en: to win a seat or office that was previously held by the opposing party
vocabulary.excerpt: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
vocabulary.example_cn: 这会削弱该党在今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？

vocabulary.en: to consider something carefully, especially by comparing different factors
vocabulary.excerpt: that's the situation that we have to weigh.
vocabulary.example_cn: Itu adalah situasi yang perlu kita timbang.

vocabulary.en: to cause something to be under pressure or tension
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
vocabulary.example_cn: Adakah gencatan senjata yang tegang dapat bertahan cukup lama untuk AS menunaikan janji perjanjian jangka panjang dengan Iran?

vocabulary.en: to fulfill a promise or expectation; to provide something promised
vocabulary.excerpt: for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: AS menunaikan janji perjanjian jangka panjang dengan Iran.

vocabulary.en: the person or thing that is most likely to win a competition or election
vocabulary.excerpt: Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado.
vocabulary.example_cn: Kenali pendahulu dalam pemilihan gabenor Republikan Colorado.

vocabulary.cn: Gelembung (merujuk kepada kenaikan harga aset yang pesat tanpa asas kukuh)
vocabulary.en: a situation in which the price of an asset rises far above its real value, often followed by a sudden collapse
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: Menunjukkan kepada saya bahawa gelembung kecerdasan buatan akan pecah.

vocabulary.en: to burst or break open suddenly, often with a sound
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: Gelembung kecerdasan buatan akan pecah.

vocabulary.en: to secure a nomination or qualification, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
vocabulary.example_cn: Graham Plattner mendapat pencalonan.

vocabulary.en: to finally win or achieve something, especially after a struggle
vocabulary.excerpt: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent.
vocabulary.example_cn: Tidak lama selepas mengunci pencalonan, dia mengalihkan perhatian kepada lawan barunya.

vocabulary.en: lacking courage or determination; weak-willed
vocabulary.excerpt: she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: Dia menjadi sama pengecut dan korup seperti establishment yang kini dia layani.

vocabulary.en: the group of people who have power and influence in a society or organization, often seen as conservative or resistant to change
vocabulary.excerpt: as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: Establishment yang dia layani.

vocabulary.cn: Suara bebas (merujuk kepada individu yang tidak terikat dengan parti politik dan menyuarakan pendapat secara bebas)
vocabulary.en: a person or entity that expresses opinions independently, not aligned with a particular party or group
vocabulary.excerpt: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
vocabulary.example_cn: Jika anda seorang suara bebas, mengapa 95% masa anda mengundi sama dengan Donald Trump?

vocabulary.word: have a dog in the fight
vocabulary.en: to have a personal stake or interest in a particular situation or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
vocabulary.example_cn: Anda mempunyai kepentingan dalam pilihan raya ini.

vocabulary.en: at risk; something that could be lost or gained
vocabulary.excerpt: what's at stake here.
vocabulary.example_cn: Apa yang dipertaruhkan di sini.

vocabulary.en: to put something at risk of being lost or harmed
vocabulary.excerpt: Maybe this has jeopardized it.
vocabulary.example_cn: Mungkin ini telah membahayakannya.

vocabulary.cn: kemenangan besar; pilihan raya berat sebelah
vocabulary.en: an easy or decisive victory, especially in an election or sports event
vocabulary.excerpt: A lot of times it can be a blowout, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: Selalunya ia mungkin kemenangan besar, bukan?

vocabulary.en: fundamentally; in its most important aspects
vocabulary.excerpt: we're saying the same thing in essence, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: Kita pada dasarnya bercakap tentang perkara yang sama, bukan?

vocabulary.en: difficult to defeat or overcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Susan Collins is tough to beat.
vocabulary.example_cn: Suzanne Collins sukar untuk dikalahkan.

vocabulary.cn: versi lemah Susan Collins (calon yang berhaluan sama tetapi lebih sederhana)
vocabulary.en: a candidate who is similar to Susan Collins but less extreme or less effective
vocabulary.excerpt: you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light.
vocabulary.example_cn: Anda tidak boleh memenangi kerusi ini dengan memilih versi lemah Susan Collins.

vocabulary.en: to motivate and rally the core supporters of a political party or candidate
vocabulary.excerpt: You are going to beat her by energizing your base.
vocabulary.example_cn: Anda perlu mengalahkannya dengan menggerakkan pangkalan sokongan anda.

vocabulary.cn: pilihan raya pertengahan penggal (pilihan raya Kongres yang diadakan di tengah-tengah penggal presiden AS)
vocabulary.en: an election held in the middle of a president's term, typically for Congress and state offices
vocabulary.excerpt: particularly in a midterm election.
vocabulary.example_cn: Terutamanya dalam pilihan raya pertengahan penggal.

vocabulary.en: a direct vote by the electorate on a specific proposal or issue
vocabulary.excerpt: this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次选举必须是对苏珊·柯林斯的公投。

vocabulary.en: to require someone to explain their actions and accept responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她拒绝追究唐纳德·特朗普的责任。

vocabulary.en: consecutively; one after another
vocabulary.excerpt: I want to play two clips back-to-back.
vocabulary.example_cn: Saya mahu memainkan dua klip secara berturut-turut.

vocabulary.en: a strong and enthusiastic statement of support
vocabulary.excerpt: these are not ringing endorsements.
vocabulary.example_cn: Ini bukan sokongan padu.

vocabulary.en: without her consent or desire
vocabulary.excerpt: who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will.
vocabulary.example_cn: Dia berkata dia mengundi Susan Collins bertentangan dengan kehendaknya sendiri.

vocabulary.en: the moral and ethical qualities of a person
vocabulary.excerpt: It's really about the character quality.
vocabulary.example_cn: Ini sebenarnya tentang kualiti peribadi.

vocabulary.en: a close and strong relationship
vocabulary.excerpt: Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond.
vocabulary.example_cn: Collins dan Trump, hubungan tidak rapat.

vocabulary.en: a mechanism that initiates a process or event
vocabulary.excerpt: The 60 days is a trigger.
vocabulary.example_cn: 60 hari adalah satu titik pencetus.

vocabulary.en: formal consent or authorization by the U.S. Congress
vocabulary.excerpt: the president has to obtain congressional approval.
vocabulary.example_cn: Presiden mesti mendapatkan kelulusan Kongres.

vocabulary.en: found guilty of a crime by a court of law
vocabulary.excerpt: individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers.
vocabulary.example_cn: Seseorang yang disabitkan menyerang polis dengan ganas.

vocabulary.en: the act of paying back money spent or lost
vocabulary.excerpt: should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: Berhak mendapat pembayaran balik kos guaman mereka.

vocabulary.en: charges for legal services provided by a lawyer or law firm
vocabulary.excerpt: reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: Pembayaran balik kos guaman mereka.

vocabulary.cn: pemotongan (biasanya merujuk kepada belanjawan atau perbelanjaan)
vocabulary.en: reductions in spending, especially in government budgets
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: Bimbang tentang pemotongan Medicaid.

vocabulary.cn: Medicaid (insurans kesihatan yang disediakan oleh kerajaan persekutuan dan negeri AS untuk golongan berpendapatan rendah)
vocabulary.en: a U.S. government program that provides health insurance to low-income individuals and families
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: Bimbang tentang pemotongan Medicaid.

vocabulary.cn: Peningkatan penguatkuasaan; tindakan penguatkuasaan yang dipertingkatkan
vocabulary.en: a sudden increase in law enforcement activities, often targeting specific issues
vocabulary.excerpt: a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minne...
vocabulary.example_cn: Hentikan sementara peningkatan penguatkuasaan di Maine dan Minnesota.

vocabulary.en: a weapon that is thrown or fired through the air, especially one that is guided
vocabulary.excerpt: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again.
vocabulary.example_cn: Peluru berpandu sekali lagi menerangi langit Timur Tengah.

vocabulary.en: a temporary agreement to stop fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough?
vocabulary.example_cn: Adakah gencatan senjata yang tegang itu dapat bertahan cukup lama?

vocabulary.cn: Dokumen Epstein (merujuk kepada dokumen kes berkaitan penjenayah seks yang telah meninggal dunia, Jeffrey Epstein)
vocabulary.en: documents related to the case of Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted sex offender
vocabulary.excerpt: a big day for the Epstein files.
vocabulary.example_cn: Hari penting dokumen Epstein.

vocabulary.en: a person who has been convicted of a sexual crime
vocabulary.excerpt: the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.example_cn: Penjenayah seks yang telah disabitkan dan meninggal dunia.

vocabulary.cn: berkaitan dengan gabenor; berkaitan dengan pilihan raya gabenor
vocabulary.en: relating to a governor or the election of a governor
vocabulary.excerpt: Colorado gubernatorial candidate.
vocabulary.example_cn: Calon gabenor Colorado.

vocabulary.en: Initial Public Offering; the first sale of a company's shares to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs.
vocabulary.example_cn: OpenAI dan Anthropic merancang untuk Tawaran Awam Permulaan.

vocabulary.en: a situation where an economic bubble is about to burst
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: Gelembung kecerdasan buatan akan pecah.

vocabulary.en: to indicate or show something, often indirectly
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: Menunjukkan kepada saya bahawa gelembung kecerdasan buatan akan pecah.

vocabulary.en: a helicopter that has been shot down or forced to crash
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: Selepas helikopter ditembak jatuh, Amerika menyerang Iran.

vocabulary.en: to attack or strike, especially militarily
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: Selepas helikopter ditembak jatuh, Amerika menyerang Iran.

vocabulary.en: a person who makes laws; a member of a legislature
vocabulary.excerpt: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links?
vocabulary.example_cn: Apakah yang akan diberitahu oleh Bill Gates kepada penggubal undang-undang tentang hubungannya dengan Epstein?

vocabulary.en: connections or relationships between people or things
vocabulary.excerpt: his links to the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他与已故的已定罪性犯罪者的联系。

vocabulary.en: to have committed murder when one was a child
vocabulary.excerpt: a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child.
vocabulary.example_cn: 一位还声称自己小时候杀过人的候选人。

sentences.en: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
sentences.cn: 缅因州的民主党人投票支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Democrats in Maine）+ 谓语（vote）+ 不定式短语（to stick with Graham Platner）作宾语。
sentences.analysis: stick with 意为"坚持支持"，是新闻常用短语。

sentences.en: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
sentences.cn: 这会让该党失去今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？
sentences.structure: 疑问句：情态动词（Could）+ 主语（that）+ 谓语（cost）+ 间接宾语（the party）+ 直接宾语（a shot at flipping the Senate this fall）。
sentences.analysis: cost sb. a shot at sth. 意为"使某人失去做某事的机会"；flip the Senate 指"翻转参议院（控制权）"，是政治新闻常用表达。

sentences.en: If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
sentences.cn: 如果选票上的选择是在那个（指普拉特纳的争议行为）和一位投票剥夺数百万美国人医保的参议员之间，那就是我们必须权衡的情况。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If...）+ 主句（that's the situation）+ 定语从句（that we have to weigh）。
sentences.analysis: weigh 意为"权衡"，是抽象动词；take health care away from 是新闻中常见的负面政策描述。

sentences.en: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
sentences.cn: 紧张的停火协议能否持续足够长的时间，让美国兑现与伊朗长期承诺的协议？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句：Will + 主语（the strained ceasefire）+ 谓语（last）+ 状语（long enough）+ 不定式短语（for the U.S. to deliver...）。
sentences.analysis: strained ceasefire 意为"紧张的停火"；long-promised 是复合形容词，意为"长期承诺的"。

sentences.en: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
sentences.cn: 比尔·盖茨将就他与已故被定罪性犯罪者的关联向立法者说些什么。
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句：What + 助动词（will）+ 主语（Bill Gates）+ 谓语（tell）+ 间接宾语（lawmakers）+ 介词短语（about his links...）。
sentences.analysis: the late convicted sex offender 指"已故被定罪的性犯罪者"，late 意为"已故的"，convicted 是过去分词作定语。

sentences.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
sentences.cn: 你认为你成年后杀过人吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句（Adakah anda fikir）+ 宾语从句（bahawa anda telah membunuh orang sebagai dewasa）。
sentences.analysis: as an adult 意为"sebagai dewasa"，与后文"as a child"形成对比。

sentences.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
sentences.cn: OpenAI 和 Anthropic 计划在今年年底前进行首次公开募股，这向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。
sentences.structure: 主句（OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs...）+ 现在分词短语（signaling to me that...）作结果状语，其中包含宾语从句（that the A.I. bubble is about to pop）。
sentences.analysis: IPO 是 Initial Public Offering 的缩写；signal to sb. that... 意为"memberi isyarat kepada seseorang bahawa..."；be about to pop 意为"akan pecah"。

sentences.en: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support.
sentences.cn: 对于任何感到失望、沮丧或幻想破灭的人，我的工作是赢得你们的信任、信念和支持。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（To any of those...）作状语，其中包含定语从句（who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned）；主句（it is my job to earn...）。
sentences.analysis: let down 意为"kecewa"；disillusioned 意为"hilang ilusi"；it is my job to do sth. 是强调结构的常见表达。

sentences.en: And I will spend every day of this campaign.
sentences.cn: 我将花费这场竞选的每一天。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（I）+ 谓语（will spend）+ 宾语（every day of this campaign）。
sentences.analysis: spend 后接时间名词，表示"menghabiskan masa"。

sentences.en: And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
sentences.cn: 如果我有幸每天在美国参议院做同样的事。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if I have the privilege...），其中包含现在分词短语（doing exactly that）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: have the privilege doing sth. 意为"有幸做某事"；the United States Senate 是专有名词。

sentences.en: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
sentences.cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Graham Platner）+ 谓语（punches）+ 宾语（his ticket）。
sentences.analysis: punch one's ticket 是习语，意为"获得（政治）提名或资格"。

sentences.en: But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator.
sentences.cn: 但现在他必须说服选民选择他而不是一位连任五届的参议员。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（he）+ 谓语（'s got to convince）+ 宾语（voters）+ 不定式短语（to choose him over...）。
sentences.analysis: convince sb. to do sth. 意为"说服某人做某事"；choose A over B 意为"选择A而不是B"；five-term 是复合形容词，意为"连任五届的"。

sentences.en: Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three-fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills.
sentences.cn: 民主党人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，尽管有所有丑闻，还是赢得了大约四分之三初选选民的支持，超过了州长珍妮特·米尔斯。
sentences.structure: 主语（Democrat Graham Platner）+ 插入语（despite all the scandals）+ 谓语（earned）+ 宾语（about three-fourths of primary voters' support）+ 状语（over Governor Janet Mills）。
sentences.analysis: despite 表示让步；three-fourths 是分数表达；over 在此表示"超过"。

sentences.en: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
sentences.cn: 在获得提名后不久，他就将注意力转向了他的新对手，共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。
sentences.structure: Kata keterangan waktu (Moments after clinching the nomination) + klausa utama (he turned his attention to...), dengan objek (his new opponent) diikuti oleh aposisi (Republican Senator Susan Collins).
sentences.analysis: clinch the nomination berarti "mendapatkan nominasi"; turn one's attention to berarti "mengalihkan perhatian ke".

sentences.en: Susan Collins may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
sentences.cn: Susan Collins mungkin memulai kariernya di Washington dengan niat baik puluhan tahun lalu, tetapi dia telah menjadi lemah dan korup seperti kemapanan yang sekarang dia layani.
sentences.structure: Kalimat majemuk: klausa pertama (Susan Collins may have started...) + but menghubungkan klausa kedua (she has become...), yang mengandung struktur perbandingan (as...as...) dan klausa relatif (she now serves).
sentences.analysis: may have started menunjukkan dugaan tentang masa lalu; spineless berarti "tidak punya pendirian"; the establishment berarti "kemapanan".

sentences.en: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
sentences.cn: Jika Anda adalah suara independen, mengapa 95% dari waktu Anda memilih sama dengan Donald Trump?
sentences.structure: Klausa kondisi (If you are...) + klausa utama (why do you vote...).
sentences.analysis: independent voice berarti "suara independen"; vote with sb. berarti "memilih sama dengan seseorang".

sentences.en: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
sentences.cn: Platner memiliki banyak pekerjaan yang harus dilakukan untuk mendapatkan dukungan pemilih Mills.
sentences.structure: Kalimat sederhana: subjek (Platner) + predikat (has) + objek (a lot of work) + frasa infinitif (to do to get...) sebagai atribut dan keterangan tujuan.
sentences.analysis: get sb. on one's side berarti "mendapatkan dukungan seseorang".

sentences.en: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
sentences.cn: Saya tidak bisa dengan jujur mendukung Platner, meskipun beberapa hal yang dia katakan memang bagus.
sentences.structure: Ayat utama (I couldn't honestly support Platner) + klausa konsesi (even though some of the things...), yang mengandungi klausa relatif (that he says).
sentences.analysis: honestly ialah kata adverba, menerangkan support; even though memperkenalkan klausa konsesi.

sentences.en: I don't think he's ready to be a senator.
sentences.cn: Saya rasa dia belum bersedia untuk menjadi senator.
sentences.structure: Ayat utama (I don't think) + klausa objek (he's ready to be a senator).
sentences.analysis: Pemindahan nafi: ayat utama nafi, klausa afirmatif.

sentences.en: I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet.
sentences.cn: Saya cenderung kepada Collins, melainkan lelaki itu menunjukkan sesuatu yang lain, tetapi saya hanya -- dia belum sampai ke tahap itu.
sentences.structure: Ayat majmuk: klausa pertama (I'm leaning towards Collins unless...) + but menghubungkan klausa kedua (I just -- he's not there yet).
sentences.analysis: lean towards bermaksud "cenderung kepada"; unless memperkenalkan klausa syarat; he's not there yet ialah ungkapan lisan, bermaksud "dia belum mencapai standard".

sentences.en: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
sentences.cn: Jadi, jika November adalah Platner lawan Collins, apa yang akan anda lakukan?
sentences.structure: Ayat tanya khas (what will you do...) + klausa syarat (if it's Platner versus Collins).
sentences.analysis: its sepatutnya it's, kesilapan ejaan; versus bermaksud "lawan".

sentences.en: I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine.
sentences.cn: Saya seorang Demokrat, tetapi saya telah mengundi Susan Collins beberapa kali kerana saya fikir dia benar-benar mengambil berat tentang Maine.
sentences.structure: Ayat majmuk: klausa pertama (I'm a Democrat) + but menghubungkan klausa kedua (I voted for Susan Collins several times), diikuti klausa keterangan sebab (because I think...), yang mengandungi klausa objek (that she does care...).
sentences.analysis: does menunjukkan penekanan; care about bermaksud "peduli tentang".

sentences.en: But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
sentences.cn: Tetapi ini akan menjadi masalah sebenar bagi saya pada bulan November.
sentences.structure: Ayat mudah: subjek (it) + predikat ('s going to be) + predikat (a real problem) + keterangan (for me in November).
sentences.analysis: be going to menunjukkan masa depan; a real problem bermaksud "masalah sebenar".

sentences.en: And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
sentences.cn: Max, saya sebenarnya akan mulakan dengan awak, kerana awak bersama VoteVets, yang bermaksud awak ada kepentingan dalam perlumbaan ini.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (I'm actually going to start with you) + klausa keterangan sebab (because you're with VoteVets), diikuti klausa relatif tak terhad (which means...), yang mengandungi klausa objek (you guys had a dog in the fight).
sentences.analysis: have a dog in the fight ialah simpulan bahasa, bermaksud "ada kepentingan".

sentences.en: Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
sentences.cn: Peluang Demokrat untuk menukar kerusi Senat Maine.
sentences.structure: Frasa nama: subjek (Democrats' chances) + frasa sendi (of flipping the Maine Senate seat) sebagai penerang.
sentences.analysis: flip the seat bermaksud "menukar kerusi (mengubah kawalan parti)".

sentences.en: Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals?
sentences.cn: Memandangkan Graham bergelut dengan skandal ini, adakah perlumbaan ini sedekat yang anda semua harapkan?
sentences.structure: Ayat tanya umum (Is this race as close as...) + Struktur perbandingan (as...as...) + Klausa objek (you all hoped it would be) + Klausa keterangan sebab (now that...).
sentences.analysis: as close as bermaksud "sama rapat dengan"; now that bermaksud "memandangkan".

sentences.en: The race is always going to be close.
sentences.cn: Perlumbaan ini sentiasa akan menjadi sangat rapat.
sentences.structure: Ayat mudah: Subjek (The race) + Predikat (is always going to be) + Predikat (close).
sentences.analysis: be going to menunjukkan masa depan; close di sini bermaksud "seimbang".

sentences.en: Not with a five-term senator, it's not.
sentences.cn: Bukan berdepan dengan senator yang berkhidmat lima penggal, bukan.
sentences.structure: Ayat ringkas: Bentuk lengkap ialah "It is not close with a five-term senator."
sentences.analysis: five-term ialah kata adjektif majmuk; it's not ialah ungkapan ringkas.

sentences.en: What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race.
sentences.cn: Apa yang saya ingin katakan tentang kempen Susan Collins ialah, apa yang kita lihat pada masa lalu ialah, anda tidak akan memenangi kerusi ini dengan memilih "Susan Collins versi ringan" atau melancarkan "Susan Collins versi ringan" dalam kempen.
sentences.structure: Klausa subjek (What I would say...) + Kata kerja penghubung (is) + Klausa predikat (that we've seen...), di mana klausa predikat mengandungi satu lagi klausa predikat (is you're not going to win...).
sentences.analysis: Susan Collins light bermaksud "versi lemah Susan Collins", light bermaksud "ringan"; put forward bermaksud "mengemukakan".

sentences.en: You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
sentences.cn: Anda akan mengalahkannya dengan memotivasikan pengundi asas anda, terutamanya dalam pilihan raya pertengahan penggal.
sentences.structure: Ayat mudah: Subjek (You) + Predikat (are going to beat) + Objek (her) + Keterangan cara (by energizing your base) + Unsur sisipan (particularly in a midterm election).
sentences.analysis: energize your base bermaksud "memberi semangat kepada pengundi setia anda"; midterm election merujuk kepada "pilihan raya pertengahan penggal".

topics.title: Pilihan Raya Senator Maine: Platner vs. Collins
topics.content: Dalam pemilihan utama Parti Demokrat di Maine, Graham Platner yang dibelenggu skandal menewaskan Gabenor Janet Mills dengan kira-kira 75% undi, dan akan mencabar Senator Republikan penyandang lima penggal, Susan Collins. Platner perlu meraih sokongan penyokong Mills, manakala Collins masih mempunyai kelebihan dalam kalangan pengundi bebas. Parti Demokrat berharap untuk merampas kerusi ini, tetapi isu peribadi Platner mungkin mempengaruhi pilihan raya.
topics.keywords: Graham Platner · Susan Collins · primary · flip · independent voter

topics.title: Serangan Tentera AS ke atas Iran
topics.content: AS melancarkan serangan peluru berpandu ke atas Iran selepas sebuah helikopter ditembak jatuh, meningkatkan ketegangan di Timur Tengah. Sebelum ini, terdapat perjanjian yang telah lama dijanjikan antara AS dan Iran, tetapi gencatan senjata (ceasefire) kini berdepan tekanan. Peristiwa ini mungkin menjejaskan kestabilan rantau dan dasar luar AS.
topics.keywords: missile · Iran · ceasefire · U.S. military strike · helicopter downed

topics.title: IPO Syarikat AI dan Kebimbangan Gelembung
topics.content: OpenAI dan Anthropic merancang untuk mengadakan Tawaran Awam Permulaan (IPO) sebelum akhir tahun, menimbulkan kebimbangan bahawa gelembung kecerdasan buatan (A.I. bubble) akan pecah. Pelabur bimbang sama ada penilaian industri AI terlalu tinggi dan sama ada IPO akan menandakan titik perubahan industri.
topics.keywords: IPO · OpenAI · Anthropic · A.I. bubble · pop

topics.title: Kenyataan Kontroversi Calon Gabenor Colorado
topics.content: Calon gabenor Republikan Colorado, Victor Marx, mendakwa pernah membunuh seseorang semasa kecil dan bertanya "Adakah itu penting?", mencetuskan kontroversi. Beliau adalah pendahulu (frontrunner) dalam pemilihan utama Republikan, dan kenyataan ekstremnya mungkin mempengaruhi pilihan raya.
topics.keywords: Victor Marx · gabenor · pendahulu · kontroversi · membunuh seseorang

quiz.question: Dalam 'Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner', maksud 'stick with' yang paling hampir ialah?
quiz.explanation: 'stick with' ialah frasa kerja yang bermaksud "terus menyokong atau memilih", di sini merujuk kepada keputusan Demokrat untuk terus menyokong Platner. Pilihan lain adalah makna literal atau berlawanan.

quiz.question: Dalam 'the A.I. bubble is about to pop', maksud 'pop' ialah?
quiz.explanation: 'pop' di sini bermaksud "pecah", merujuk kepada gelembung yang pecah. Dalam konteks ekonomi, 'bubble pops' sering digunakan untuk menunjukkan gelembung meletus.

quiz.question: Dalam 'Graham Platner punches his ticket', apakah maksud 'punches his ticket'?
quiz.explanation: 'punch one's ticket' ialah simpulan bahasa yang bermaksud "mendapat pencalonan atau kelayakan", terutamanya dalam konteks politik yang menunjukkan kejayaan mara ke peringkat seterusnya.

quiz.question: Berdasarkan teks, apakah cabaran utama yang dihadapi oleh Platner selepas memenangi pilihan raya utama?
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebut bahawa Platner perlu "get Mills voters on his side", iaitu mendapatkan sokongan pengundi Mills, kerana Mills tewas dalam pilihan raya utama dan pengundinya mungkin beralih kepada Collins.

quiz.question: Manakah antara berikut benar tentang Susan Collins?
options: A. Beliau mempunyai hubungan rapat dengan Trump
B. Beliau adalah senator lima penggal
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebut Collins sebagai "five-term senator", iaitu senator lima penggal. Beliau tidak mempunyai hubungan rapat dengan Trump ('not a tight bond'), dan merupakan seorang Republikan.

quiz.question: Apakah pandangan yang dipetik dalam teks mengenai IPO AI?
options: B. Penilaian syarikat AI adalah munasabah
C. IPO akan memacu perkembangan AI
quiz.explanation: Pandangan yang dipetik dalam teks ialah "the A.I. bubble is about to pop", iaitu gelembung AI akan pecah, menunjukkan IPO mungkin isyarat puncak gelembung.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner. Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall? REP. ALEXANDRIA OCASIO-CORTEZ (D-NY): If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: Hari ini dalam sembang kumpulan, Demokrat Maine mengundi untuk Graham Platter. Adakah ini akan menyebabkan mereka kehilangan peluang untuk membalikkan Senat pada musim luruh ini? Ahli Dewan Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (Demokrat New York): Jika pilihan pada undi adalah antara itu dan seorang senator yang mengundi untuk melucutkan penjagaan kesihatan berjuta-juta rakyat Amerika, itulah yang perlu kita timbang.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again. Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long- promised deal with Iran? And a big day for the Epstein files. What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
paragraphs.cn: Peluru berpandu sekali lagi menerangi langit Timur Tengah. Bolehkah gencatan senjata yang rapuh bertahan cukup lama untuk AS menunaikan perjanjian komitmen jangka panjang dengan Iran? Hari penting untuk fail Epstein. Apakah yang akan dikatakan Bill Gates kepada penggubal undang-undang mengenai hubungannya dengan penjenayah seks yang disabitkan dan telah meninggal dunia?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: Adakah anda fikir anda pernah membunuh seseorang semasa dewasa? Victor Marx (Republikan),

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child. Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado. And it's the $3.5 trillion question on Wall Street. Has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself?
paragraphs.cn: Ini adalah calon yang mendakwa pernah membunuh semasa kecil. Kenali pendahulu dalam pemilihan utama Republikan untuk gabenor Colorado. Ini adalah soalan $3.5 trilion Wall Street. Adakah kegilaan AI sudah terlalu awal?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: OpenAI dan Anthropic merancang untuk mengadakan tawaran awam permulaan pada akhir tahun ini, yang menunjukkan kepada saya bahawa gelembung AI akan pecah. Graham Platter (Demokrat),

paragraphs.speaker: Maine Senate Candidate
paragraphs.text: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support. And I will spend every day of this campaign. And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
paragraphs.cn: Bagi sesiapa yang berasa kecewa atau terpedaya, tugas saya adalah untuk mendapatkan kepercayaan, keyakinan, dan sokongan anda. Saya akan menghabiskan setiap hari dalam kempen ini. Jika saya bernasib baik untuk berada di Senat AS, saya akan melakukannya setiap hari.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Graham Platner punches his ticket. But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator. Good morning everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Here is where we begin. That big statement out of Maine. Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three- fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills. Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Graham Platter memenangi pencalonan. Tetapi sekarang dia perlu meyakinkan pengundi untuk memilihnya daripada seorang senator yang telah berkhidmat selama lima penggal. Selamat pagi semua. Saya Audi Cornish. Mari kita mulakan. Kenyataan besar dari Maine. Graham Platter, Demokrat, walaupun terlibat skandal, memenangi sokongan kira-kira tiga perempat pengundi utama, mengalahkan Gabenor Janet Mills. Tidak lama selepas mendapat pencalonan, dia mengalihkan perhatian kepada lawan barunya, Senator Republikan Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Platner
paragraphs.text: Susan Collins may have started -- may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves. If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
paragraphs.cn: Susan Collins mungkin mempunyai niat baik ketika memulakan kerjayanya di Washington beberapa dekad lalu, tetapi dia telah menjadi tidak berprinsip dan korup seperti pertubuhan yang kini dia layani. Jika anda adalah suara bebas, mengapa anda mengundi untuk Donald Trump 95% masa?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
paragraphs.cn: Platter perlu melakukan banyak kerja untuk mendapatkan penyokong Mills menyebelahinya.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
paragraphs.cn: Saya benar-benar tidak boleh menyokong Platter, walaupun beberapa perkara yang dia katakan memang baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I don't think he's ready to be a senator. I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet. And --
paragraphs.cn: Saya tidak fikir dia sudah bersedia untuk menjadi senator. Saya cenderung untuk menyokong Collins, melainkan lelaki itu menunjukkan sesuatu yang lain, tetapi saya hanya -- -- dia belum sampai ke tahap itu. Dan -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, jika November adalah antara Plattner dan Collins, apa yang akan anda lakukan?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: That's a really good question. I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine. But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
paragraphs.cn: Itu soalan yang bagus. Saya seorang Demokrat, tetapi saya telah mengundi Susan Collins beberapa kali kerana saya fikir dia benar-benar mengambil berat tentang Maine. Tetapi November akan menjadi masalah sebenar bagi saya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, today in the group chat, Mike Leon, host of the "Can We Please Talk" podcast; Jared Stepman, columnist with "The Daily Signal"; and Max Rose, former Democratic congressman from New York. And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race. You guys have been giving a lot of money across the country. And what is it? Let me just show what -- what's at stake here. Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, hari ini dalam sembang kumpulan, ada hos podcast 'Can We Talk?', Mike Leone, kolumnis 'The Daily Signal', Jared Steppman, dan bekas wakil Demokrat New York, Max Rose. Max, saya sebenarnya nak tanya awak dulu, kerana awak bekerja di VoteVets, yang bermaksud awak juga ada kepentingan dalam perlumbaan ini. Awak telah melabur banyak wang di seluruh negara. Jadi, bagaimana keadaannya? Biar saya tunjukkan -- -- apa yang penting di sini. Peluang Demokrat untuk menawan semula kerusi Senat Maine.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It was pretty good. Maybe this has jeopardized it. We're going to show people a chart while you're talking so you can see what's at stake. Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals? MAX ROSE (D),
paragraphs.cn: Pada asalnya agak baik. Mungkin ini telah terjejas. Kami akan tunjukkan carta kepada orang ramai semasa awak bercakap, supaya mereka nampak apa yang dipertaruhkan. Memandangkan Graham bergelut dengan skandal ini, adakah perlumbaan ini sedekat yang awak semua harapkan? Max Rose (Demokrat):

paragraphs.speaker: Former New York Congressman
paragraphs.text: The race is always going to be close. Because --
paragraphs.cn: Perlumbaan ini akan sentiasa dekat. Kerana -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Not with a five-term senator, it's not. A lot of times it can be a blowout, right? Like what -- how do you look at what he needs to accomplish?
paragraphs.cn: Bagi seorang senator yang memegang jawatan selama lima penggal, tidak begitu. Selalunya mungkin berat sebelah, kan? Seperti -- -- bagaimana awak lihat apa yang dia perlu capai?

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: Well, I think that we're saying the same thing --
paragraphs.cn: Hmm, saya rasa kita bercakap tentang perkara yang sama -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: -- in essence, right? Susan Collins is tough to beat. What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race. You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- pada dasarnya, kan? Susan Collins sukar dikalahkan. Apa yang saya nak katakan tentang kempen Susan Collins ialah, pada masa lalu kita lihat, awak tidak boleh menang dengan memilih 'Susan Collins versi ringan' atau melancarkan 'Susan Collins versi ringan' dalam perlumbaan. Awak kalahkan dia dengan memberi inspirasi kepada pengundi asas awak, terutamanya dalam pilihan raya pertengahan penggal.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: There is no doubt, though, that this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins: on the ways in which she has voted to empower Donald Trump; on her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable; on the ways in which she has said, I might be fair, but I want to take healthcare away from millions of people. Says the right thing often; does the wrong thing very frequently.
paragraphs.cn: Namun, tidak syak lagi, pilihan raya ini mesti menjadi referendum terhadap Susan Collins: tentang cara dia mengundi untuk memberi kuasa kepada Donald Trump; tentang cara dia enggan meminta pertanggungjawaban Donald Trump; tentang cara dia berkata, saya mungkin adil, tetapi saya mahu mengambil penjagaan kesihatan daripada berjuta-juta orang. Dia selalu cakap betul; tetapi sangat kerap buat perkara yang salah.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, this is very much the message we hear over and over again. And I love that Platner in front of him, it said, "They don't know Maine." You know what I mean? Usually not his own name, not a bunch of other things. It was like, hello, media, this one is for you. I want to play two clips back-to-back, because it sort of, I think, represents something of this race. First, I'm going to play for you J.D. Vance and how he talks about Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Jadi, itulah mesej yang kita dengar berulang kali. Saya suka papan tanda Plattner di hadapannya yang tertulis 'Mereka tidak faham Maine'. Awak faham maksud saya? Biasanya bukan namanya sendiri, atau benda lain. Macam, media, ini untuk awak. Saya nak mainkan dua klip audio berturut-turut, kerana saya rasa ini mewakili beberapa ciri perlumbaan ini. Pertama, saya akan mainkan klip J.D. Vance bercakap tentang Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then I'm going to play AOC and how she's talking about Platner. You tell me how you hear the tone of these. J.D. VANCE (R),
paragraphs.cn: Kemudian saya akan mainkan klip AOC bercakap tentang Plattner. Awak beritahu saya bagaimana awak rasakan nada-nada ini. J.D. Vance (Republikan):

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. Vice President
paragraphs.text: Here's the thing I'll say about Susan Collins, is sometimes I get frustrated with Susan Collins. I almost wish that she was more partisan. But the thing I love about Susan is she is independent, because Maine is an independent state. And frankly, if she was as partisan as I sometimes wish that she was, she would not be a good fit for the people of Maine. So, let's give a shout-out to Susan Collins, who's doing a great job.
paragraphs.cn: Apa yang saya nak katakan tentang Susan Collins ialah, kadang-kadang saya rasa kecewa dengannya. Saya hampir berharap dia lebih partisan. Tetapi apa yang saya suka tentang Susan ialah, dia bebas, kerana Maine adalah negeri yang bebas. Secara jujur, jika dia partisan seperti yang saya kadang-kadang harapkan, dia tidak akan sesuai untuk rakyat Maine. Jadi, mari kita beri tepukan kepada Susan Collins, dia buat kerja yang baik.

paragraphs.speaker: Ocasio-Cortez
paragraphs.text: When it comes to the substance of this reporting, obviously, there's a lot in that behavior that's really challenging. It's hard to stomach, you know, in some of it. But -- but at the end of the day, I think that this is a choice. If that choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take healthcare away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: As for the substance of this report, obviously, there is a lot that is very disturbing in that behavior. Some of it is hard to accept. But ultimately, I think it's a choice. If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who would strip millions of Americans of their healthcare, that's the situation we have to weigh.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I played these, because one is like, it's hard to stomach. And the other is like, I get frustrated. Shout-out though, anyway, because I guess Maine. And I was, like, these are not ringing endorsements. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?"
paragraphs.cn: I played these because one is hard to accept and the other frustrates me. But still, applause, because I think it's Maine. I thought, these are not enthusiastic endorsements. Mike Leon, host, "Can we talk about it?"

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: But Audie, not only are they not ringing endorsements, the voter that we played in the package before listen to her tone. Maine independents are the second largest party in Maine.
paragraphs.cn: But Audi, not only is it not an enthusiastic endorsement, listen to the tone of that voter we played in the earlier clip. Independents in Maine are the second largest party in the state.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And a woman, in particular.
paragraphs.cn: Yes, especially women.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: And a woman and a Democrat who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will, it sounded like, from that thing. So -- but those are -- those are some of the things that we're going to have to consider now. It's really about the character quality is what something that Graham Platner has been trying to say: Forget about that. I'm from here. It's how she's voted, to Max's point.
paragraphs.cn: A female Democrat said she voted for Susan Collins, but it sounded like against her will. So -- -- but these are some of the things we have to consider now. It's really about character traits, which is what Graham Plattner has been trying to say: forget that. I'm from here. What matters is her voting record, as Max said.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: But then also you hear the voters, and they're, like, a little, still unsure because of those character issues with Platner. So, it's going to be really interesting.
paragraphs.cn: But you also hear voters, they are still a bit uncertain because of Plattner's character issues. So, it will be very interesting.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And he's been telling a story for a while of, like, you know, I was -- went through difficult times. Now I'm a person who has changed. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond. OK? Can I just put it that way? Here's a list I'm going to play for you. Just some moments over the past couple of months where you hear her talking around Trump policies and where she stands on them. SEN. SUSAN COLLINS (R-ME): The 60 days is a trigger.
paragraphs.cn: And he has been telling a story, like, you know, I've been through hard times. Now I'm a changed person. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, not close. Okay? Can I say that? Here's a list, I'm going to play it for you. Just some moments from the past few months where you hear her talking about Trump's policies and her stance on them. Senator Susan Collins (R-ME): 60 days is a trigger point.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: After 60 days, in my view, the president has to obtain congressional approval or Congress can block it. I do not believe that individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers on January 6th should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees. Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid and the impact on my state, but other states, as well. I've talked with the secretary of homeland security.
paragraphs.cn: After 60 days, in my view, the president must get congressional approval, or Congress can block it. I don't think people convicted of violent assault on police on January 6 should be entitled to reimbursement of legal fees. I am concerned about the impact of Medicaid cuts on my state and others. I have spoken with the Secretary of Homeland Security.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have strongly recommended to her that there be a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minnesota.
paragraphs.cn: I strongly advised her to suspend enforcement actions in Maine and Minnesota.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, I've chased her in the hallways before, too. She often ends up voting with Republicans, whatever that party line is. But is Trump going to get involved in this simply because of what's at stake with the Senate? Or is he going to send surrogates? He'll be like, OK, J.D., you go there? Like, can she expect any support there? JARRETT STEPMAN, COLUMNIST, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes, I feel that's why he sent J.D. and I feel he will, if he's smart, actually keep some distance from this.
paragraphs.cn: I've chased her in the hallway before. She often ends up voting with Republicans, regardless of party lines. But will Trump get involved just because of the Senate stakes?
